system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors

        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.pimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
keyword div(phi,k) is undefined in dictionary "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run1/runs/pitzDaily/3/system/fvSchemes/divSchemes"

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run1/runs/pitzDaily/3/system/fvSchemes/divSchemes from line 24 to line 27.

    From function const Foam::entry& Foam::dictionary::lookupEntry(const Foam::word&, bool, bool) const
    in file db/dictionary/dictionary.C at line 831.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword div(phi,k) is undefined in dictionary ".../system/fvSchemes/divSchemes"
```

The `pimpleFoam` solver requires a divergence scheme for `div(phi,k)`, but the current `fvSchemes` file only defines `div(phi,epsilon)` — it's missing `div(phi,k)`.

Looking at the history: In Attempt 1, the same type of error occurred for `div(phi,epsilon)`. That was fixed by adding `div(phi,epsilon) Gauss linear;`. Now `div(phi,k)` produces the same kind of error, meaning the fix for one scalar field exposes the next missing one.

## Root Cause

The `pitzDaily` tutorial (and `pimpleFoam` in general) transports both `k` (turbulent kinetic energy) and `epsilon` (turbulence dissipation rate). Both need explicit divergence scheme entries in `divSchemes`. The current `fvSchemes` has `div(phi,epsilon)` but is still missing `div(phi,k)`.

## Proposed Solution

Add the missing divergence scheme entry to the `divSchemes` sub-dictionary in **`system/fvSchemes`**:

```
divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss linear;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(k);
}
```

This adds `div(phi,k)` with an appropriate upwind scheme (`Gauss linearUpwind grad(k)`), consistent with how `div(phi,U)` is handled. Alternatively, a simpler `default none;` combined with explicit entries for each transported scalar field would also work — but since `default none` is already set, just adding the two missing explicit entries (`div(phi,k)` and `div(phi,epsilon)`) is the cleanest approach. Wait — `div(phi,epsilon)` is already there from the previous fix. So only `div(phi,k)` needs to be added now.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/fvSchemes', 'changes': 'Add div(phi,k) Gauss linearUpwind grad(k) entry to divSchemes sub-dictionary'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-04.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
